9:T30a7,<div class="specification">
<p>A random variable <em>Z</em> is normally distributed with mean 0 and standard deviation 1. It is known that P(<span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
</math></span> &lt; −1.6) = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="a" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>a</mi>
</math></span> and P(<span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
</math></span> &gt; 2.4) = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="b" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>b</mi>
</math></span>. This is shown in the following diagram.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>A second random variable <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="X" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>X</mi>
</math></span> is normally distributed with mean <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="m" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>m</mi>
</math></span> and standard deviation <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="s" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>s</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
<p>It is known that P(<span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
</math></span> &lt; 1) = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="a" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>a</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Find P(−1.6 &lt; <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
</math></span> &lt; 2.4). Write your answer in terms of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="a" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>a</mi>
</math></span> and <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="b" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>b</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Given that <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
</math></span> &gt; −1.6, find the probability that z &lt; 2.4 . Write your answer in terms of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="a" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>a</mi>
</math></span> and <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="b" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>b</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
<div class="marks">[4]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Write down the standardized value for <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x = 1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
</math></span>.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>It is also known that P(<span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
</math></span> &gt; 2) = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="b" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>b</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
<p>Find <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="s" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>s</mi>
</math></span>.</p>
<div class="marks">[6]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div>a:T2667,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p style="color: #999;font-size: 90%;font-style: italic;">* This question is from an exam for a previous syllabus, and may contain minor differences in marking or structure.</p><p>recognizing area under curve = 1        <em><strong>(M1)</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg   </em><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="a + x + b = 1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>a</mi>

<mo>+</mo>

<mo>+</mo>
<mi>b</mi>
</math></span></p>

<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="{\text{P}}\left( {z &gt;  - 1.6} \right) = 1 - a" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

</math></span> (seen anywhere)        <em><strong>(A1)</strong></em></p>

<p><em><strong>[4 marks]</strong></em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z = - 1.6" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
<mo>=</mo>

<mn>1.6</mn>
</math></span> (may be seen in part (d))     <em><strong>A1  N1</strong></em></p>
<p><strong>Note:</strong> Depending on the candidate’s interpretation of the question, they may give <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{1 - m}}{s}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<mi>z</mi>
<mo>=</mo>

<mn>1.6</mn>
</math></span> as part of their working in part (d), the <em><strong>A1</strong> </em>in part (c) may be awarded.</p>
<p><em><strong>[1 mark]</strong></em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>attempt to standardize <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="x" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>x</mi>
</math></span> (do not accept <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{x - \mu }}{\sigma }" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

</mrow>

</mfrac>

<mfrac>

<mo>−</mo>

</mrow>
<mi>s</mi>

<mi>m</mi>

<mi>σ</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span></p>
<p>correct equation with each <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="z" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>z</mi>
</math></span>-value      <em><strong>(A1)(A1)</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg</em>   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext=" - 1.6 = \frac{{1 - m}}{s}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mo>−</mo>
<mn>1.6</mn>
<mo>=</mo>
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>1</mn>
<mo>−</mo>
<mi>m</mi>
</mrow>
<mi>s</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span>,  <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="2.4 = \frac{{2 - m}}{s}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mn>2.4</mn>
<mo>=</mo>
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>2</mn>
<mo>−</mo>
<mi>m</mi>
</mrow>
<mi>s</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span>,  <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="m + 2.4s = 2" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>m</mi>
<mo>+</mo>
<mn>2.4</mn>
<mi>s</mi>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>2</mn>
</math></span></p>
<p>valid approach (to set up equation in one variable)       <em><strong>M1</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg   </em><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="2.4 = \frac{{2 - \left( {1.6s + 1} \right)}}{s}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mn>2.4</mn>
<mo>=</mo>
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>2</mn>
<mo>−</mo>
<mrow>
<mo>(</mo>
<mrow>
<mn>1.6</mn>
<mi>s</mi>
<mo>+</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
</mrow>
<mo>)</mo>
</mrow>
</mrow>
<mi>s</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span>,   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{1 - m}}{{ - 1.6}} = \frac{{2 - m}}{{2.4}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mn>1</mn>
<mo>−</mo>

<mn>1.6</mn>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
<mo>=</mo>

<mrow>
<mn>2</mn>
<mo>−</mo>
<mi>m</mi>
</mrow>
<mrow>
<mn>2.4</mn>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
</math></span></p>
<p>correct working        <em><strong>(A1)</strong></em></p>
<p>eg   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="1.6s + 1 = 2 - 2.4s" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mn>1.6</mn>
<mi>s</mi>
<mo>+</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>2</mn>
<mo>−</mo>
<mn>2.4</mn>
<mi>s</mi>
</math></span>,  <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="4s = 1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mn>4</mn>
<mi>s</mi>
<mo>=</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
</math></span>,  <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="m = \frac{7}{5}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>m</mi>
<mo>=</mo>

<mn>7</mn>
<mn>5</mn>
</mfrac>
</math></span></p>
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="s = \frac{1}{4}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>s</mi>
<mo>=</mo>
<mfrac>
<mn>1</mn>
<mn>4</mn>
</mfrac>
</math></span>       <em><strong>A1 N2</strong></em></p>
